verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine with no intrinsic transmembrane domain. It lacks a membrane anchor of its own. However, the latent TGF-β1 complex (LAP-TGF-β1) is stably presented on the extracellular face of the plasma membrane in specific cell types — on regulatory T cells via GARP (LRRC32) and on macrophages/microglia via NRROS (LRRC33), both transmembrane proteins. This surface tethering is wash-resistant and non-covalent but stable, exposing the LAP and mature TGF-β1 domains to the outer leaflet. Integrin-mediated activation of this surface pool is well characterized. Recent therapeutic mAbs (Cell Reports, 2025) explicitly target membrane-bound latent TGF-β1 on Tregs, confirming surface accessibility. The dominant form is secreted/ECM-deposited, making surface presentation cell-state and cell-type restricted rather than universal.
